stories.headline: Empowering Journalists with Statistics
stories.summary: The article explores the application of statistical techniques in journalism, aiming to enhance the field of computer-assisted reporting. It emphasizes the importance of understanding and leveraging statistical methods like linear and logistic regression, as well as reliability analysis, to make data-driven journalistic stories.

stories.headline: SAS vs. SPSS: Playing with the big guns
stories.summary: Statistical analysis software packages SAS and SPSS are expensive and complex tools with steep learning curves, but they provide powerful capabilities for advanced data analysis in computer-assisted reporting when routine methods prove insufficient.

stories.headline: Looking at police stats: It ought to be a crime
stories.summary: Jeff Green of The Oakland Press bypassed the FBI to independently collect crime statistics directly from 60 local law enforcement agencies across Oakland County, Michigan, creating a comprehensive database that scooped competitors.

stories.headline: A look at widgetmaking: Economic analysis on the fly
stories.summary: The article explains how location quotients serve as a crucial analytical tool for journalists to objectively measure whether a region's perceived economic strengths, such as manufacturing or financial services, are actually concentrated compared to national averages.
